If you find yourself vacant, distracted, and antisocial in airports, on freeways, and, increasingly, university classes, which is not supposed to happen if you’re meant to be there,

then don’t beat yourself up, these places are where we are least human and most cog

simply being shuffled from one place to another

which is not a bad thing if you wanna get to Cancun!

Usually I habitually ignore announcements except for their factual information,

But I just fully paid attention to the flight announcement

And it made my face flash red with anger for a second

For how condescending United Airlines the company was being to me

I formally have a contractual relationship with United

But that doesn’t mean we don’t feel things for each other

Aren’t we both supposed to respect basic polite etiquette as rights-bearing equal members of a republic?!!

intelligence, complexity, life, love

the yogis were lonely pioneers into a new continent that all of us are in the process of crashing into

some of us will evolve

personal and transcendent at the same time

you will have a personal relationship with the planet, and perhaps, if you're lucky, the universe

I understand. Thanks for engaging so openly.

My immediate reaction is that I understand where you're coming from, this is your war to fight, and I wish you the best of luck. I had a similar but ultimately different war to fight, I got to the end of it, and now I am done. We crossed paths along the way. I am an Indian American former liberal with a different destiny, karma, and dharma from you, a fully Western German American, if I remember correctly. Having suffered enough from our mutual enemy, end of history leftism, I truly wish you the best.

My second observation is

@HaikuHeroics that you are probably seeing a more skewed representation of bloodlusty leftists than there are in reality because of social media. You can probably find plenty of neutral/opponent liberals if you want to who you could live in the same society with. I know tons. My mom said…

that you are probably seeing a more skewed representation of bloodlusty leftists than there are in reality because of social media. You can probably find plenty of neutral/opponent liberals if you want to who you could live in the same society with. I know tons. My mom said whatabouttheschoolshootings to Charlie Kirk and that annoyed me, but I know that she's a totally normal suburban woman. A lot of this is still structural and you can be friends with leftists.

But that's neither interesting nor useful. I'd like to wish you more than just good luck, and share some relevant parts of my journey.

I've almost certainly had a very similar life to you, spiritually if not literally...

@HaikuHeroics Actually I'll keep this reply short and just dm you. Basically fmp your friend/enemy distinction is true and important because it leads you to courage and bravery in the name of building something positive. Yes, the darkness and depravity you see in the left is really there. I…

Actually I'll keep this reply short and just dm you. Basically fmp your friend/enemy distinction is true and important because it leads you to courage and bravery in the name of building something positive. Yes, the darkness and depravity you see in the left is really there. I think you can feel good about making yourself ready to meet the challenge. 

But it's also true that the world is not made of a spectrum from friends to enemies. The world is not divided in this way. The entire world is you and has something to teach you, including leftists. So the darkness and depravity you are certain you see in leftists is in some way an illusion that reveals something about you you haven't seen before. You can harden yourself now, but you won't truly win the war unless you fully surrender the victory. That's because the war will transform you and your enemy in ways you don't know yet.

Being both Caesar and Christ is not really about showing mercy. It's not about mercy because that means you don't see the value in the evil of leftists. What it's really about is fighting the battle while surrendering wanting to win from within because you don't know that you are good and they are evil, or who you are. That basically means that I think the war between you and leftists is good and meaningful and the immense pain you and your friends and Charlie Kirk have felt and will feel is good and meaningful and I hope you have the courage to feel it fully. This war and its pain has something to teach both you and the leftists. That doesn't mean there isn't good or evil. I hope you win. This is beyond good and evil.

It's good and important to fight to build order. This is what I wanted to do the whole time. I had enemies who were in the way of this order and I wished they would be gone. When I was fully ready to die, metaphorically and literally, when I got to the point where I didn't know anything including my life, I found out that it's all been okay the whole time. The battle is important but nothing is more important than peace and love. Love is more powerful than all enemies, it is the source of all order, beyond all order, so you'd best love your enemy. Don't have mercy on them, love them as you destroy them. 

The easy way is to face these battles with courage and surrender from within, making yourself your enemy. This is why I say the entire world is you, and there are no enemies. How do leftists make you feel? Can you love that feeling? If you can, you still have to fight the leftists do your duty, but isn't it a lot easier?

I want you to have an easy way because I think I understand some of who you are, and I relate to it deeply. I have lived a very similar life to you. My battles are over now though. I suspect that you will go the hard way by thinking that fighting this battle without is ultimately important. I respect your pagan beliefs and think its an important part of whatever comes next, but I suspect that they overemphasize courage in battle, and don't understand that surrender is ultimately superior. I think you probably started this journey because you were looking for love, and I'll just say in order to find it you may have to love your enemy. It would be funny if your future wife or something ends up being a leftist pagan and love has that sense of humor.

I'm not a Christian btw. I used to be a Hindu, then an ahteist American liberal, and now basically just tpot, which I guess is just hinduism. But I'm not really any of these things, I just destroyed myself to find love, and when I was ready to die, I did.

US employer based private health insurance exists because of Uncle Sam’s liberal good intentions

The IRS decided to exempt health insurance benefits from being taxed in 1943 after wage controls were imposed to prevent inflation from the war effort

The system locked into a tight relationship between employers, employees, and state/federal government because of this historical event, it evolved from then on unabated

The IRS’s decision, then under FDR, represents great liberal governance at the height of American industrial society— it was gently putting workers on health insurance and forcing employers to support it at a time when the nation was under pressure from the war

This made sense at a time when you’d work for the same company your whole life— in the atomized, polarized, lonely, socially networked America of 2025, it no longer makes sense

Health insurance costs are the hidden costs of loneliness— of the mismatch between the digital world where most people form connections, and the calcified, industrial shape of physical American society

It’s like America is frozen in time, stuck in the past back to when we proved our greatness— we can’t move forward and we are paying the price

Loneliness results not from social media but from the lack of connection between social media and life in the physical world

Health is that connection— nothing is more real than the inevitability of death

We pay the price because decisions about what to cover (what a health insurance policy is) are not made by real communities that care for themselves

(Plus whatever the fuck is going on with drug prices— I need help with that one :) @RuxandraTeslo 

America can no longer shoulder the burden of 1943, we are no longer responsible for the world

We have done our job and we did it well— we can stop paying for it

We must let go of our past, repair our torn social infrastructure, and form the foundations of our future

I believe that an open source social media network that connects care providers with individuals and individuals with communities that can insure themselves is the future of free society

We can finally realize the full promise of Silicon Valley’s vision from the very beginning— building an open source constitution for a truly democratic society

We can finally resolve the core contradiction of our nation— between freedom and equality

Let’s automate the United States Constitution, make Thomas Jefferson proud, and relieve Uncle Sam of his burden
Work in progress @DefenderOfBasic

What is charisma?

Free expression is not just about thought but also emotion

Those who are capable of wide emotional expression are most able to handle the intensity of free expression. Free speech selects for these people.

For children and those who aren’t ready for the highest intensity arenas, we can have “zones” of intimacy. These are gradients of intimacy in a society with free expression. This is how it already works naturally.

In order to be able to say anything anywhere, you also need to be able to be personal.

Otherwise you MUST accept the possibility of a gradation of “violent” free expression, ranging from verbal to ultimately physical. This is okay and acceptable because true, stable free expression isn’t possible without this latent possibility.

“Violent” free expression, like verbal attacks, but also physical attacks, can be profound and sophisticated. It can have a positive impact on the quality of speech. It can also be base and degrade the information environment.

Sophisticated speakers must be CAPABLE of violent expression, and must develop their own style of “weaponry”. This is different from name calling because it can create MORE order by creating chaos. It’s complex.

But in the end it is far superior to speak from love, and be capable of being personal to everyone.

Transcendent and personal at the same time. Emulating divine reality.

THAT is charisma.

Methodology—

Instead of analyzing the world to build systems,

I look at anything and everything as a timeless whole as it presents itself to me (ie w as little personal ego as I can manage), and then, from love, I see if I can See and Feel how much of it is real and how much is an illusion

I hold on to no judgments or insights, my mind serves just like an extra eye, integrating information into a hazy picture. I’m not sure if this is related to the “third eye” thing at all, I’m just describing my experience
